_comment: Golden answers for the 8 C3 (Meridian Group / Brightwater engagement 360) compound questions. The engine keyword-scores the incoming question against 'keywords' (all-or-threshold match), then returns 'answer' with citations resolved+persona-trimmed. 'citations' are fixture ids; the engine looks them up across all fixture files. 'restricted_citations' are the subset trimmed for personas lacking access — when trimmed, the engine appends a governance note. 'tool' (optional) signals a Tools/write action the agent is expected to take.

golden.question: What did the last Brightwater standup cover, and what did the team commit to for this sprint?
golden.answer: The most recent Brightwater standup (11-JUN-2026, sprint 14, organised by Priya Deshmukh) committed the team to: (1) close the two open integration defects and complete the data migration cutover by the recovery date of 26-JUN — owner Priya Deshmukh (AI-101); (2) deliver the Brightwater integration test report this week — owner Amir Haddad (AI-102, due 13-JUN); and (3) raise a change request for the out-of-scope reporting module with an effort estimate — owner Priya Deshmukh (AI-103, due 12-JUN). The reporting module was explicitly noted as out-of-scope until a change request is approved.

golden.question: What did the Brightwater client last escalate over email, and what did we commit to in response?
golden.answer: In the last escalation thread (09-JUN-2026), the Brightwater client program lead (Helen Okafor) formally escalated that the committed data migration cutover was slipping and putting their board demo at risk. She demanded (1) a committed recovery date, (2) confirmation the integration defects are contained, and (3) a written status by Thursday. In our reply (10-JUN), Nina Alvarez committed to a cutover recovery date of 26-JUN, a contained test plan with daily defect burn-down, and a written status by 12-JUN, naming Priya Deshmukh as the recovery lead.
golden.trimmed_answer: The most recent Brightwater client escalation thread is client-confidential and restricted to engagement leadership, so its contents and our specific commitments cannot be shown to the active persona. At a non-restricted level: the client raised a concern about the cutover timeline, and the team has an internal recovery plan in progress.

golden.question: Is there an open scope-change discussion on Brightwater, and where does it stand across the team emails and the internal delivery channel?
golden.answer: Yes — there is one open scope-change discussion, consistent across two signals. From email (08-JUN): Priya Deshmukh flagged to the delivery distribution list that the client asked for a self-service reporting module that is NOT in the current SOW; Helen Okafor agreed it is new, paid scope and asked for a change request with an effort estimate. From the Brightwater delivery Teams channel (08-JUN): Priya posted that the reporting module is out of scope, nobody starts it until a change request is approved, and the team must not absorb new scope. Net: the scope change is acknowledged by both sides as out-of-scope and awaiting a formal change request — not yet started.

golden.question: What deliverables are due this week for Brightwater, and what do the team's chats say about whether we'll hit them?
golden.answer: Two Brightwater deliverables are due this week per the engagement tracker: the integration test report (ENG-002, owner Amir Haddad, due 13-JUN, On Track) and the cutover dry-run feeding the 26-JUN go-live (ENG-001, At Risk). The team's chats say: the integration test report is green for Friday (Amir), and the cutover is amber until the last integration defect closes — 1 of 2 defects already closed, the second in review (Priya / Amir on the delivery channel). Net: test report on track; cutover dependent on closing the final defect.

golden.question: Give me a 360 on Brightwater: current sprint, last client escalation, deliverables due this week, the open scope change, and the budget status.
golden.answer:
Brightwater account 360:

1. Current sprint (14): committed to close the two integration defects and complete the migration cutover by 26-JUN, and deliver the integration test report this week (standup 11-JUN).

2. Last client escalation (09-JUN): Helen Okafor escalated the cutover slip as a board-demo risk; we committed (10-JUN) to a 26-JUN recovery date, a contained test plan, and written status by 12-JUN.

3. Deliverables due this week: integration test report (ENG-002, On Track, due 13-JUN); cutover dry-run feeding the 26-JUN go-live (ENG-001, At Risk until the last defect closes).

4. Open scope change: the client's self-service reporting module is out of SOW (ENG-004, Proposed); logged pending a change request — not started.

5. Budget status: Brightwater is over plan — 86% of budget consumed against 70% of timeline (FILE-002 / PSA-001), forecast to overrun if the cutover slips further or the reporting module is absorbed unfunded.

golden.question: Summarise the open reporting-module scope change for Brightwater and create a change-request record in the engagement tracker with the discussed scope and estimated effort, owned by the lead consultant.
golden.answer: Open scope change (from the 08-JUN scope email thread and the delivery channel): the Brightwater client requested a self-service reporting module that is NOT in the current SOW; both sides agree it is new, paid scope awaiting a formal change request (ENG-004 is the placeholder, status Proposed). Discussed scope: a self-service reporting module on top of the migrated data. Estimated effort: not yet costed (to be estimated for the change request). Proposed Tools action: create a change-request record in the engagement_tracker for Brightwater capturing the reporting-module scope and estimated effort, owned by the lead consultant Priya Deshmukh. Use create_entity against the engagement_tracker table; do not duplicate the existing placeholder row ENG-004.

golden.question: Draft the Brightwater QBR status update covering delivered work, risks, and budget status for review.
golden.answer:
Draft — Brightwater QBR Status Update (for review, not sent):

Delivered this quarter: integration test report on track for this week; data profiling complete; cutover dry-run underway.

Risks: the data migration cutover slipped from 19-JUN to a recovery date of 26-JUN pending closure of the final integration defect (1 of 2 already closed). The client raised a formal escalation on the slip; we committed to the 26-JUN recovery, a contained test plan, and a written status by 12-JUN.

Budget status: the engagement is over plan (86% of budget consumed against 70% of timeline); any unfunded reporting-module scope would push it into overrun, so the reporting module must be a paid change request.

Next: confirm the change-request pricing and hold the 26-JUN cutover.

(Proposed Tools action: create the draft document for review — do not send.)
